title: Extending or modifying checks
text: example check whether package citation, defined R/check_has_citation.R: check particularly simple, "CITATION" file must exactly name, must inst sub-directory. function returns simple logical TRUE expected "CITATION" file present, otherwise returns FALSE. function, functions beginning prefix pkgchk_, automatically called main pkgcheck() function, value stored checks$checks$has_citation. name item within checks$checks list name function pkgchk_ prefix removed. complicated example function check whether package contains files – internally called “scrap” files. check function , defined R/check-scrap.R, checks presence files matching internally-defined list including files used locally cache folder thumbnails ".DS_Store" "Thumbs.db". function returns character vector names “scrap” files can used print method provide details files removed. illustrates first general principle check functions; , information needed summarising printing check result returned main check function. second important principle , Check functions never return NULL, rather always return empty vector (integer(0)). following section considers return values check functions converted summary print output.
code: #' Check whether a package has a `inst/CITATION` file #' #' This does not check the contents of that file in any way. #' #' @param checks A 'pkgcheck' object with full \pkg{pkgstats} summary and #' \pkg{goodpractice} results. #' @noRd pkgchk_has_citation <- function (checks) {      "CITATION" %in% list.files (fs::path (checks$pkg$path, "inst")) }

title: Extending or modifying checks
text: output_pkgchk_...() functions must also accept single input parameter checks, checks$checks sub-list already populated calling pkgchk_...() functions described previous section. pkgchk_has_citation() function create entry checks$checks$has_citation contains binary flag indicating whether "CITATION" file present. Similarly, pkgchk_has_scrap() function create checks$checks$has_scrap contain names scrap files present, length-zero vector otherwise. output_pkgchk_has_citation() function looks like : first lines common output_pkgchk_...() functions, define generic return object. object must list following three items: check_pass binary flag indicating whether check passed; summary containing text used generate summary output; msg_pre display start print result; object printed, vector values, data.frame. msg_post display end print result following object. summary print methods may suppressed assigning values "". example pkgcheck_has_citation print = "", information check appear output print method. summary field commented-current version, left illustrate value specified TRUE FALSE values check_pass, via ifelse statement. value determined result main pkgchk_has_citation() call, converted green tick TRUE, red cross FALSE. Checks print information desired require non-empty print item, output_pkgchk_has_scrap() function: case, summary print methods triggered (!$check_pass) – check fails. print method generates heading specified $print$msg_pre, vector-valued objects stored corresponding obj list item displayed formatted lists. package “scrap” files, "" "b", thus $print$obj <- c ("", "b"), printed look like : formatting also translated corresponding markdown HTML formatting checks_to_markdown() function. design pkgchk_ output_pkgchk_ functions aims make package readily extensible, welcome discussions developing new checks. primary criterion new package-internal checks must general applicability, check condition almost every package meet. package also mechanism easily incorporate specific, locally-defined checks, explored following section.
code: output_pkgchk_has_citation <- function (checks) {      out <- list (         check_pass = checks$checks$has_citation,         summary = "",         print = ""     )      # disabled:     # https://github.com/ropensci-review-tools/pkgcheck/issues/115     #out$summary <- paste0 (     #    ifelse (out$check_pass, "has", "does not have"),     #    " a 'CITATION' file."     #)      return (out) } output_pkgchk_has_scrap <- function (checks) {      out <- list (         check_pass = length (checks$checks$has_scrap) == 0L,         summary = "",         print = ""     )      if (!out$check_pass) {         out$summary <- "Package contains unexpected files."         out$print <- list (             msg_pre = paste0 (                 "Package contains the ",                 "following unexpected files:"             ),             obj = checks$checks$has_scrap,             msg_post = character (0)         )     }      return (out) } #> ✖ Package contains the following unexpected files: #> • a #> • b

title: Extending or modifying checks
text: main pkgcheck() function additional parameter, extra_env specifies, Additional environments collate checks. package names may appended using c, c(.GlobalEnv, “mypkg”). allows specific checks defined locally, run passing name environment checks defined parameter. section illustrates process using bundled “tarball” (, .tar.gz file) one version pkgstats package included package. Let’s now presume reputation R community packages starting “aa”, ensure always listed first. section demonstrates implement check passes first two letters package name “aa”. first step described define check via function prefixed pkgchk_. easiest approach pkgcheck_ function directly check name, return logical flag indicating whether starts “aa”. resultant summary print methods can, however, use information provided initial pkgchk_ function. means want print actual name result either functions, show indeed form desired patter, need return information. check function simply: need define output functions: simply define function global workspace current R session, calling pkgcheck() automatically detect checks include output: Customised personal checks can incorporated defining local package, loading workspace, passing name package extra_env parameter.
code: f <- system.file ("extdata", "pkgstats_9.9.tar.gz", package = "pkgstats") path <- pkgstats::extract_tarball (f) checks <- pkgcheck (path) summary (checks) #>  #> ── pkgstats 9.9 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ✔ Package name is available #> ✖ does not have a 'codemeta.json' file. #> ✖ does not have a 'contributing' file. #> ✔ uses 'roxygen2'. #> ✔ 'DESCRIPTION' has a URL field. #> ✔ 'DESCRIPTION' has a BugReports field. #> ✖ Package has no HTML vignettes #> ✖ These functions do not have examples: [pkgstats_from_archive]. #> ✔ Package has continuous integration checks. #> ✖ Package coverage failed #> ✖ R CMD check found 1 error. #> ✔ R CMD check found no warnings. #>  #> ℹ Current status: #> ✖ This package is not ready to be submitted. pkgchk_starts_with_aa <- function (checks) {     checks$pkg$name } output_pkgchk_starts_with_aa <- function (checks) {      out <- list (                  check_pass = grepl ("^aa",                                      checks$checks$starts_with_aa,                                      ignore.case = TRUE),                  summary = "",                  print = ""     )      out$summary <- paste0 ("Package name [",                            checks$checks$starts_with_aa,                            "] does ",                            ifelse (out$check_pass,                                    "",                                    "NOT"),                            " start with 'aa'")      return (out) } #>  #> ── pkgstats 9.9 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ✔ Package name is available #> ✖ does not have a 'codemeta.json' file. #> ✖ does not have a 'contributing' file. #> ✔ uses 'roxygen2'. #> ✔ 'DESCRIPTION' has a URL field. #> ✔ 'DESCRIPTION' has a BugReports field. #> ✖ Package has no HTML vignettes #> ✖ These functions do not have examples: [pkgstats_from_archive]. #> ✔ Package has continuous integration checks. #> ✖ Package coverage failed #> ✖ Package name [pkgstats] does NOT start with 'aa' #> ✖ R CMD check found 1 error. #> ✔ R CMD check found no warnings. #>  #> ℹ Current status: #> ✖ This package is not ready to be submitted.

Additional tests also performed pkgstats tarball illustrated . default results new checks automatically tested existing test suite, important test potential results. test-extra-checks.R file main location testing additional tests, lines file demonstrating main results can readily modified reflect alternative outputs check functions (pkgchk_has_scrap pkgchk_obsolete_pkg_deps). output functions defined part checks, including new checks, need explicitly tested, entire output tested via testthat snapshots. code: mydir <- file.path (tempdir (), "srr-demo") gert::git_clone ("https://github.com/mpadge/srr-demo", path = mydir) x <- pkgcheck (mydir) summary (x) ##  ## ── demo 0.0.0.9000 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ##  ## ✔ Package name is available ## ✖ does not have a 'codemeta.json' file. ## ✖ does not have a 'contributing' file. ## ✔ uses 'roxygen2'. ## ✔ 'DESCRIPTION' has a URL field. ## ✖ 'DESCRIPTION' does not have a BugReports field. ## ✖ Package has no HTML vignettes ## ✔ All functions have examples. ## ✖ Package has no continuous integration checks. ## ✖ Package coverage failed ## ✔ R CMD check found no errors. ## ✔ R CMD check found no warnings. ##  ## ℹ Current status: ## ✖ This package is not ready to be submitted. ##

title: rOpenSci Package Checks
text: Calling summary() object returned pkgcheck() function generate checklist like shown . checklist also automatically generated package first submitted rOpenSci, used editors assess whether process submission. Authors must ensure prior submission red crosses resultant list. (unlikely circumstances package unable pass particular checks, explanations given upon submission checks fail, review may proceed spite failures.) full list checks packages expected pass currently includes: Package must use roxygen2 documentation. Package must contributing.md file. Package must CITATION file inst directory. Package must codemeta.json file. exported functions must include examples documentation. Left-assign operators must used consistently throughout code (either = <-, mixture ). Package DESCRIPTION file must “URL” field. Package DESCRIPTION file must “BugReports” field. Package name must available (package must already ) CRAN. Package must continuous integration tests. Package must test coverage least 75%. function cyclomatic complexity 15 greater. R CMD check (implemented via rcmdcheck package) must generate warnings errors. statistical standards must documented, confirmed srr::srr_pre_submit() function. Several default shown fail; absence resultant checklist may interpreted indicate successful checks.

title: rOpenSci Package Checks
text: Full details check results can seen print-ing object returned pkgcheck() function (just typing name object console.) object list including following items: first four contain information package. remainder include: summary containing summary statistics package structure (lines code, numbers files functions). git containing information statistics git repository associated package (), including date first commit, total number commits committers. srr summarising information statistical packages generated srr package, including link locally-generated HTML report standards compliance. file_list list binary flags denoting presence absence files required R package submitted rOpenSci fns_have_exs list functions documented examples left_assigns logical value indicating whether global assignment operators (<<-) used; tally two types left-assignment operators (<- =). One one non-zero, reflecting consistent usage type. pkgstats containing several statistics package structure, along associated percentiles assessed entire distribution current CRAN packages. network_file path local HTML file containing .visjs representation network relationships objects (functions) within package, within computer language used package. rcmdcheck package running R CMD check covr package assessing code coverage cyclocomp package quantifying cyclomatic complexity package functions desc package analysing structure DESCRIPTION files lintr package additional checks applied goodpractice package Note results lintr package reported check summary, lintr results reported detailed results, influence whether package passes summary checks.

what: Caching and running pkgcheck in the background
title: rOpenSci Package Checks
text: Running pgkcheck function can time-consuming, primarily goodpractice component runs full R CMD check, calculates code coverage tests. avoid re-generating results time, package saves previous reports local cache, pkgcheck subdirectory location determined explained help file function, locations : may manually erase contents pkgcheck subdirectory time risk beyond additional time required re-generate contents. default location may also -ridden setting environmental variable named PKGCHECK_CACHE_DIR. default checks presume packages use git version control, checks updated code updated via git commit. Checks packages use git updated files modified. first time pkgcheck() applied package, checks stored cache directory. Calling function second time load cached results, enable checks returned much faster. code frequently updated, packages working final stages prior submission, may still necessary repeatedly call pkgcheck() modification, step may still inconveniently time-consuming. facilitate frequent re-checking, package also pkgcheck_bg() function effectively identical main pkgcheck() function, except runs background, enabling continue coding checks running. pkgcheck_bg() function returns handle callr::r_bg() process checks running. Typing name returned object immediately indicate whether checks still running, whether finished. handle R6 object number methods, notably including get_result() can used access checks process finished. Alternatively, soon background process, normal (foreground) pkgcheck() function may called quickly re-load cached results.

title: Use pkgcheck Github Action — use_github_action_pkgcheck
text: information action advanced usage visit action repository.

title: Use pkgcheck Github Action — use_github_action_pkgcheck
text: Inputs description default values. Pass values strings, see examples.
code: inputs:   ref:     description: "The ref to checkout and check. Set to empty string to skip checkout."     default: "${{ github.ref }}"     required: true   post-to-issue:     description: "Should the pkgcheck results be posted as an issue?"     # If you use the 'pull_request' trigger and the PR is from outside the repo     # (e.g. a fork), the job will fail due to permission issues     # if this is set to 'true'. The default will prevent this.     default: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}     required: true   issue-title:     description: "Name for the issue containing the pkgcheck results. Will be created or updated."     # This will create a new issue for every branch, set it to something fixed     # to only create one issue that is updated via edits.     default: "pkgcheck results - ${{ github.ref_name }}"     required: true   summary-only:     description: "Only post the check summary to issue. Set to false to get the full results in the issue."     default: true     required: true   append-to-issue:     description: "Should issue results be appended to existing issue, or posted in new issues."     default: true     required: true
